series_title: Abbot Nikon Vorobyov: Letters to Spiritual Children
editorial_policy: Use Nikon Vorobyov letters as spiritual-counsel source material, not as full-text public-domain assumptions.
Do not reproduce complete modern translations unless reuse rights are verified.
Prefer paraphrase, short attributed excerpts, and original narration around the counsel.
Frame counsel reverently as Orthodox pastoral guidance; avoid sensational claims.
Each produced video must pass Saints visual QC: at least one verified icon/portrait, monastery/context photo, manuscript/source image, and per-scene real-image coverage.

core_sources.title: Nikon (Vorobiov) - OrthodoxWiki
core_sources.usable_for: Biographical outline, dates, priesthood, persecution-era context, letters reputation.
core_sources.rights_note: Use as reference; verify primary details against additional Orthodox sources.

core_sources.rights_note: Modern editions/translations likely copyrighted; use for sourcing and rights review.

preferred_visuals: portrait or icon of Abbot Nikon Vorobyov if license verified
Russian church/monastery context from public-domain or CC sources
book cover/source page only if rights-safe
handwritten manuscript or old letter imagery with verified license
period Russian church/persecution-era context photos where licensing is clear
visual_source_requirements.generated_visuals: allowed only as short atmospheric fill after real-image coverage is met

episodes.working_title: The Letter That Teaches Peace of Heart
episodes.hook: A persecuted Russian priest wrote to his spiritual children that peace of heart was not optional comfort, but the place where God is present.
story_beats: Introduce Abbot Nikon and the world that formed his letters.
Explain why letters became pastoral care across distance and suffering.
Center the counsel on humility, forgiveness, and peace of heart.
Apply the letter carefully to modern anxiety and resentment without turning it into self-help.
End with repentance as the road back to peace.
episodes.rights_risk: medium: modern translation/repost; paraphrase and cite.

episodes.working_title: Why Are You Writing Such Despondent Letters?
episodes.hook: A spiritual daughter wrote from despondency. Abbot Nikon answered not with flattery, but with a hard mercy about suffering and hope.
story_beats: Open with the emotional force of a despondent letter.
Frame suffering in Orthodox pastoral language without cruelty.
Show Nikon as a sober spiritual father, not a sentimental comforter.
Translate the counsel into a viewer-facing meditation on endurance.
Close with prayer, patience, and refusing despair.
episodes.rights_risk: medium: modern translation/repost; paraphrase and cite.

episodes.working_title: The Choice Between the Passions and Christ
episodes.hook: In one letter, Abbot Nikon reduced the spiritual life to a terrible choice: give in to the passions, or suffer through the struggle toward Christ.
story_beats: Open with the stark choice in the letter, paraphrased not quoted.
Explain passions in plain Orthodox terms.
Show why struggle is not failure but the arena of spiritual growth.
Connect Nikon to the Russian ascetic and pastoral tradition.
End with practical repentance and watchfulness.
episodes.rights_risk: medium: modern translation/repost; paraphrase and cite.

next_action: Research episode 001 deeper, create script outline, and build visual_plan with verified icon/portrait plus real source images before render.
